meta.definition: Set is a mathematical concept used as an object, quantity, structure, or named idea in mathematical discourse.
meta.provenance_note: OntoMathPRO class mathematics:E847 has direct rdfs:label Set@en; selected as canonical IRI according to Week 2 rules
meta.accessibility_note: Speech rendering should use the canonical label and a concise definition when the notation may be ambiguous.

meta.definition: Subset is a mathematical relation used to compare or connect mathematical objects.
meta.provenance_note: OpenMath Official set1 Content Dictionary includes subset as a relation symbol; selected as canonical source because no safer OntoMathPRO exact class was confirmed
meta.accessibility_note: Speech rendering should make the relation explicit rather than relying only on symbol names.

meta.definition: Variable is a context-dependent mathematical role used inside expressions, formulas, or models.
meta.provenance_note: MathModDB contains DecisionVariable as a variable-related source IRI; selected as provisional source IRI for Variable because no clean general Variable class was found in the available seed sources
meta.accessibility_note: Speech rendering should preserve the expression context because this concept changes meaning by role.

meta.definition: Scalar is a mathematical concept used as an object, quantity, structure, or named idea in mathematical discourse.
meta.provenance_note: OpenMath experimental linalg5 Content Dictionary includes exact symbol name scalar for a scalar matrix / scalar constant times identity; selected as provisional source IRI for Scalar because no clean general Scalar class was found

meta.definition: Determinant is a mathematical operation used to transform one or more inputs into a result.
meta.provenance_note: OpenMath Official linalg1 Content Dictionary includes determinant as the unary function returning the determinant of a square matrix; selected as canonical source for Determinant
meta.accessibility_note: Speech rendering should name the operation and, where possible, identify its operands and result.

meta.definition: Derivative is a mathematical operation used to transform one or more inputs into a result.
meta.provenance_note: OpenMath Official calculus1 Content Dictionary includes diff for ordinary differentiation of a unary function; selected as canonical source because no safer general OntoMathPRO derivative class was confirmed
meta.accessibility_note: Speech rendering should name the operation and, where possible, identify its operands and result.

meta.definition: Integral is a mathematical operation used to transform one or more inputs into a result.
meta.provenance_note: OntoMathPRO class mathematics:E1307 has rdfs:label Integrating@en and a Russian comment stating that the process of finding an integral is integration; selected as the closest operation-level canonical IRI for Integral
meta.accessibility_note: Speech rendering should name the operation and, where possible, identify its operands and result.

meta.definition: Limit is a mathematical concept used as an object, quantity, structure, or named idea in mathematical discourse.
meta.provenance_note: OpenMath Official limit1 Content Dictionary includes limit for the limit of a unary function; selected as canonical source because OntoMathPRO results showed narrower theorem-related limit concepts rather than a general limit concept
meta.accessibility_note: Speech rendering should use the canonical label and a concise definition when the notation may be ambiguous.
